instruction:
Implement support for namespace prefixes in `find_scale()` and `validate_guide()` functions. When a scale or guide is specified with a `pkgname::` prefix (e.g., `"legendry::colring"` or `"pillar::num"`), the lookup should search in the specified namespace rather than searching all loaded namespaces. This allows scales and guides from non-attached packages to be found.

Specifically:
1. Modify `validate_guide()` to parse `::` prefixes and look for guides in the specified namespace
2. Modify `find_scale()` to parse `::` prefixes when processing scale types returned by `scale_type()` methods
3. Refactor `find_global()` to accept a list of environments to search
4. Add an `as_namespace()` helper that can be mocked in tests

fail_to_pass: validate_guide finds guides with namespace prefixes - should find guide when prefixed with namespace
find_scale finds scales with namespace prefixes - should find scale when scale_type returns prefixed name
pass_to_pass: find_scale gives sensible calls to scales
existing scale lookup continues to work without prefixes

key_changes: Parse `::` in guide/scale names using `strsplit(name, "::", fixed = TRUE)`
Use `as_namespace(prefix)` to get the namespace environment to search
Refactor `find_global()` to accept a list of environments instead of single environment
Add `as_namespace()` function with `on_load()` pattern for testability
Search environments in order: provided env(s), then ggplot2 namespace
potential_pitfalls: Need to handle both prefixed and non-prefixed names in the same code path
The `find_global()` refactoring must maintain backward compatibility
String parsing should use `fixed = TRUE` in `strsplit()` for performance
Need to use `get0()` instead of `exists()`/`get()` pattern for cleaner NULL handling
The `as_namespace()` function needs to be mockable for testing without loading external packages
solution.reference_diff:
diff --git a/NEWS.md b/NEWS.md
index a252538302..aab8bce821 100644
--- a/NEWS.md
+++ b/NEWS.md
@@ -1,5 +1,8 @@
 # ggplot2 (development version)
 
+* Scales and guides specified by a string can now use package name prefixes to
+  indicate a namespace wherein to look for the scale/guide. For example, one can
+  use `scale_x_continuous(guide = "legendry::axis_base")` (@teunbrand, #4705).
 * `get_layer_data()` and `get_layer_grob()` now accept layer names as index 
   (@lgaborini, #6724)
 * Added new argument `geom_curve(shape)` that will be passed down to 
diff --git a/R/guides-.R b/R/guides-.R
index 8213ff6723..584aefbbfe 100644
--- a/R/guides-.R
+++ b/R/guides-.R
@@ -915,8 +915,19 @@ include_layer_in_guide <- function(layer, matched) {
 validate_guide <- function(guide) {
   # if guide is specified by character, then find the corresponding guide
   if (is.character(guide)) {
-    fun <- find_global(paste0("guide_", guide), env = global_env(),
-                       mode = "function")
+    check_string(guide, allow_empty = FALSE)
+    search_env <- list(global_env())
+    if (isTRUE(grepl("::", guide))) {
+      guide <- strsplit(guide, "::", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
+      # Append prefix as namespaces to search environments
+      search_env <- c(search_env, list(as_namespace(guide[[1]])))
+      # Remove prefix from guide name
+      guide <- guide[[2]]
+    }
+    fun <- find_global(
+      paste0("guide_", guide),
+      env = search_env, mode = "function"
+    )
     if (is.function(fun)) {
       guide <- fun()
     }
diff --git a/R/scale-type.R b/R/scale-type.R
index e9f3b8cc9b..a217603cd0 100644
--- a/R/scale-type.R
+++ b/R/scale-type.R
@@ -7,10 +7,18 @@ find_scale <- function(aes, x, env = parent.frame()) {
   }
 
   type <- scale_type(x)
-  candidates <- paste("scale", aes, type, sep = "_")
 
-  for (scale in candidates) {
-    scale_f <- find_global(scale, env, mode = "function")
+  for (scale in type) {
+    search_env <- list(env)
+    if (isTRUE(grepl("::", scale))) {
+      scale <- strsplit(scale, "::", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
+      # Append prefix as namepaces to search environments
+      search_env <- c(search_env, list(as_namespace(scale[[1]])))
+      # Remove prefix from scale name
+      scale <- scale[[2]]
+    }
+    scale <- paste("scale", aes, scale, sep = "_")
+    scale_f <- find_global(scale, search_env, mode = "function")
     if (!is.null(scale_f)) {
       sc <- scale_f()
       sc$call <- parse_expr(paste0(scale, "()"))
@@ -29,18 +37,29 @@ find_scale <- function(aes, x, env = parent.frame()) {
 # ggplot2 namespace environment.  This makes it possible to override default
 # scales by setting them in the parent environment.
 find_global <- function(name, env, mode = "any") {
-  if (exists(name, envir = env, mode = mode)) {
-    return(get(name, envir = env, mode = mode))
+
+  if (!is.list(env)) {
+    env <- list(env)
   }
+  env <- c(env, list(as_namespace("ggplot2")))
 
-  nsenv <- asNamespace("ggplot2")
-  if (exists(name, envir = nsenv, mode = mode)) {
-    return(get(name, envir = nsenv, mode = mode))
+  for (e in env) {
+    found <- get0(name, envir = e, mode = mode)
+    if (!is.null(found)) {
+      return(found)
+    }
   }
 
   NULL
 }
 
+# This exists for testing purposes (mocking) only
+as_namespace <- function(...) NULL
+on_load({
+  as_namespace <- base::asNamespace
+})
+
+
 #' Determine default scale type
 #'
 #' You will need to define a method for this method if you want to extend
